candidates.row_label: Depreciation & Amortization in Cost of Revenues
candidates.why: Appears as a sub-component within the selected COGS total rows ('Cost of sales' / 'Cost of revenues/COGS'), supported by the adjacent balancing-value structure. Adding it separately would double count.
Likely the same COGS-related D&A concept, but the row is blank in the preview and in any case appears to be a component of the selected COGS total rather than a separate same-level expense to add.

variables.notes: Selected COGS rows are parent total cost lines. The explicit COGS D&A row appears embedded within those totals, not outside them.

candidates.why: Appears to be a component of the selected parent row 'Selling expenses', with a balancing-value row indicating decomposition. Should not be added separately.
Appears to be a component of the selected parent row 'Administrative expenses', alongside 'Other Administrative expenses'. Should not be added separately.
Appears under the selected parent row 'Other operating expenses' and behaves like a sub-component of that bucket, not a separate same-level SG&A D&A line.

variables.notes: Selected XSGA bucket consists of parent operating expense rows ('Selling expenses', 'Administrative expenses', 'Other operating expenses'). The visible D&A rows are nested components within those selected rows, so adding them separately would double count. No separate operating R&D expense row is visible, and no R&D D&A row is present to consider under the special rule.
